message:
test(tui): add shell navigation and discoverability contract tests (br-1xt0m.1.13.6)

12 new unit tests covering navigation/discoverability invariants:

Registry contracts (tui_screens/mod.rs):
- jump_key_legend_reflects_14_screens: legend includes 1-9,0,!,@,#,$
- jump_key_coverage_all_screens: every screen has a jump key
- short_labels_fit_in_tab_width: all short_labels <= 12 chars
- screen_descriptions_nonempty_and_actionable: descriptions start uppercase
- every_category_represented: all ScreenCategory variants have screens
- screen_ids_unique_in_registry: no duplicate IDs
- jump_key_labels_unique: no duplicate jump keys
- screen_from_jump_key_roundtrips: jump_key_label -> screen_from_jump_key

Keymap contracts (tui_keymap.rs):
- help_entries_no_duplicate_actions: no duplicate entries
- contextual_help_navigation_covers_all_categories: all categories in nav
- all_profiles_produce_valid_help_entries: Default/Vim/Emacs/Minimal work
- contextual_help_screen_section_uses_screen_label: screen title + tip shown

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1xt0m.1.11.1","title":"T16.11.1: Structured Diagnostic Sections for SystemHealth Text Mode","description":"Break SystemHealth text mode into structured diagnostic sections with clear priority ordering.

Acceptance: text mode is scannable and action-oriented, not monolithic.

Implementation considerations:
- Preserve full feature behavior while improving UX clarity and parity with showcase interaction quality.
- Handle edge and degraded states explicitly (narrow widths, empty data, unavailable actions, invalid mode transitions).
- Keep keyboard and mouse pathways consistent where interaction semantics overlap.

Validation and logging requirements:
- Add/update unit tests for local logic (happy path, edge cases, and error handling).
- Ensure this bead is covered by relevant T16.13 snapshot/e2e/perf tasks.
- Emit structured diagnostics in validation logs with scenario_id, step_id, assertion_id, and elapsed_ms.
- Capture before/after evidence for any visible behavior changes.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: implementation is complete, behavior is verified via unit + appropriate e2e/snapshot coverage, detailed logs are available for failures, and no UX regression is introduced in related flows.","notes":"Implementation considerations:
- Keep behavior aligned with showcase parity goals while preserving existing feature surface.
- Treat keyboard and mouse pathways as first-class where interaction applies.
- Include explicit edge/error handling (empty states, narrow widths, unavailable actions, invalid transitions).

- Add/extend unit coverage for local logic changes (happy path + edge + error).

 {"id":"br-1xt0m.1.13","title":"T16.13: Parity Regression Suite, Evidence Bundle, and Closeout","description":"Create hard quality gates for parity remediation: tests, performance, and evidence artifacts.\n\nDetailed decomposition (added in polish pass):\n- Unit matrices: T16.13.6, T16.13.7, T16.13.8\n- Snapshot matrix: T16.13.9\n- E2E script suites: T16.13.10, T16.13.11, T16.13.12\n- Logging/artifact contract: T16.13.13\n- Performance regressions: T16.13.14\n- Failure-injection E2E: T16.13.15\n- Deterministic orchestration and CI gate: T16.13.16\n- Traceability/evidence map: T16.13.17\n\nIntent: make validation as granular as implementation so closure is evidence-based, reproducible, and self-documenting.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:13:24.512722003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:59:25.773464371Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","qa","t16-parity","testing","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1xt0m.1.13","depends_on_id":"br-1xt0m.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:13:24.512722003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":642,"issue_id":"br-1xt0m.1.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Quality gate policy:
- unit tests for logic invariants,
- snapshot tests for hierarchy/spacing/width behavior,
- e2e flows for operator-critical journeys,
